elements: 3D trapezoidal camera shape in lateral translation
simple landscape scene with horizon
motion arrows on the camera lateral to its direction
small arrows above the camera representing lateral velocity V_X and V_Y
various 3D objects at different distances from the camera in the scene
apparent velocity vectors of object projections in image plane
cloud in the far background appearing stationary

interactions.teaches: Allows viewing the scene from different angles to observe lateral motion effects more clearly
Reveals information on how different depths affect apparent speeds due to lateral motion
Demonstrates parallax effect by showing objects closer to the camera moving faster compared to those further away

demo_steps.title: Initial setup and explanation
demo_steps.narration: Observe the camera moving laterally along the scene. Objects appear to move oppositely in the image plane due to this lateral movement. The apparent speed is higher for objects closer to the camera and slower for the farther ones.

demo_steps.title: Visualizing parallax
demo_steps.narration: Click to activate the camera's lateral translation. See how nearby trees appear to slide sideways quickly across the frame, while distant objects like clouds barely shift. This is the core observation of the parallax effect.

demo_steps.title: Understanding depth and apparent motion
demo_steps.narration: Hover over different velocity vectors. Notice the relationship between an object's depth in the 3D space and its apparent speed in the 2D image plane — this forms the basis for depth recovery techniques in stereo vision.

plan.camera_suggestion: Front view with a slightly elevated perspective to show the camera and scene's side view, maintaining the original aspect ratio

inline_constraints.default_view: must match the original image silhouette, size, label scale, and crop
inline_constraints.visible_ui: at most 2 compact sliders/toggles, visible near an edge with no filled panel, and must not cover important geometry
inline_constraints.explainers: hover tooltips and click popups only
inline_constraints.framing: match the source crop and whitespace; do not force-fill the iframe if the original has margins
inline_constraints.camera: infer the source camera angle/projection/zoom so the first rendered frame is a drop-in visual replacement

plan.notes: The trapezoidal camera shape must replicate the original stylized depiction. Ensure all velocity vectors are oriented correctly and spaced consistently to represent the lateral motion field accurately. Use THREE.Line to visualize motion vectors; ensure all elements maintain proper scaling and proportional relationships in 3D space.
